::: {.hero-full}
![](images/hero.jpg)
:::

::: {.home-layout}

::: {.home-sidebar}

![](images/portrait.jpg)

<div class="profile-links-vertical">

<a href="mailto:bailey.anderson@slf.ch">
<i class="bi bi-envelope-fill"></i> Email
</a>

<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=40Ppn9IAAAAJ" target="_blank">
<i class="bi bi-mortarboard-fill"></i> Google Scholar
</a>

<a href="https://www.slf.ch/en/staff/anderson/" target="_blank">
<i class="bi bi-building"></i> WSL / ETH
</a>

</div>

:::

::: {.home-main}

# Bailey J. Anderson

## Hydrologist

I seek to understand why apparently similar catchments respond differently to similar forcings, and how those responses emerge from interacting hydrological processes across space and time.

[Research](research.qmd){.button} [Download CV](cv.qmd){.button}

## Research Themes

<div class="research-grid">

<a class="research-card" href="research.html#catchment-response-diversity">
  <img src="images/diversity.jpg" alt="Catchment response diversity">
  <div>
    <h3>Catchment response diversity</h3>
    <p>Why do apparently similar catchments respond differently?</p>
  </div>
</a>

<a class="research-card" href="research.html#variability-and-whiplash">
  <img src="images/extremes.jpg" alt="Variability and whiplash">
  <div>
    <h3>Variability and Whiplash</h3>
    <p>Understanding rapid state changes in streamflow.</p>
  </div>
</a>

<a class="research-card" href="research.html#hydrological-processes">
  <img src="images/processes.jpg" alt="Hydrological processes">
  <div>
    <h3>Hydrological processes</h3>
    <p>Challenging how methodological choices influence scientific conclusions.</p>
  </div>
</a>

</div>

:::

:::
